title: task
description: rust/lichen-gateway/src/aprs_is.rs:435 - If altitude field '/A=NNNNNN' contains a number that overflows i32 (e.g., '/A=9999999999'), the parse().ok()? returns None, rejecting the entire APRS packet. An optional field parse failure should not reject a valid position packet. Should use unwrap_or(0) or clamp the value instead of propagating None.
close_reason: Fixed: clamp altitude feet-to-decimeters conversion to i16 range before casting to prevent overflow (e.g., 40,000 feet would overflow i16::MAX of ~10,749 feet equivalent)
